manifest.author: wesswart77
manifest.description: A workspace for working life: Home command centre, CRM, PRM, Planner with reminders, rich projects, Reports, and CSV import. Markdown source-of-truth, no server required.
manifest.name: Cadence

name: Cadence

releases.author: wesswart77
releases.description:
## ✨ Big update

A major contribution from **@Jumitti** (PR #4) lands a stack of new features. Thank you, Julien.

### New: dashboard analytics widgets
- New **Analytics & Charts** section on the CRM Dashboard
- Click `+ Add Custom Chart` → pick a field to group by, pick a chart style → live widget
- Four chart styles: 🍩 Donut · 📊 Bar · 🗃️ KPI Cards · 📋 List
- Switch between styles on any widget without recreating it
- Widgets persist in plugin settings

### New: custom entity properties
- Define your own frontmatter fields per entity type (Contact, Company, Deal, Project, Partner, Activity, …)
- Property types: text · multitext (chip input) · enum (dropdown) · date · tags · currency
- Autocomplete sources: vault folder lookup, history, Obsidian's global tags, or none
- Manage everything from Settings → **Custom Entities Properties**

### New: relationship Graph View in Reports
- Reports → **Graph View** — shows connections between contacts, companies, partners, projects, deals, activities
- Auto-links contacts and projects as you create entities

### New: multiple views per section
- Sub-sections can now display as Table · Kanban · Card view
- Switch view from the section header

### Other additions
- TaskNotes plugin integration — Cadence sub-blocks can host TaskNotes
- Templates system — define entity templates that drive new-item creation
- New "News" sub-section + a new sidebar **Projects** section
- Daily-note text blocks support Dataview & DataviewJS queries

### Hygiene fixes
- Removed all 4 `innerHTML` assignments — chart rendering now uses real DOM construction (better security, better Obsidian compliance)
- Fixed 116 `style:` parameters in `createDiv`/`createEl` calls that were being silently dropped by Obsidian — the analytics widgets and several settings dialogs now render with their intended layout

### Credit
This release is largely the work of [**@Jumitti** (Minniti Julien)](https://github.com/Jumitti) — +8,316 lines via PR #4. He also maintains a separate configurable fork called **BOB Workspace** built on this codebase. Worth a look if your use case calls for heavier customisation: https://github.com/Jumitti
releases.name: 0.15.0 — analytics widgets, custom entities, graph view

releases.author: wesswart77
releases.description:
## 🧹 CSS health

Removes every `!important` declaration from styles.css. The Obsidian community portal CSS lint flagged 15 of them; all came from the mobile/touch media queries added in 0.14.x.

### Fix approach
Each `!important` was replaced with a higher-specificity selector — either:
- Prefix with `.cadence-app` to bump from (0,1,0) to (0,2,0)
- Chain a class to itself for modal rules (rendered outside `.cadence-app`)

This is the same specificity pattern stylelint and the Obsidian guidelines recommend.

### What you'll see
Nothing visually — pure CSS health fix. The portal's plugin health score should tick back up next scan.
releases.name: 0.14.4 — CSS lint clean

releases.author: wesswart77
releases.description:
## 🐛 Fix

Mobile top bar was padding itself with the iOS safe-area inset on top of Obsidian's own status-bar handling — leaving a ~80px empty band under the title on iPhone. Removed our top inset; Obsidian Mobile already places the workspace correctly below the status bar / notch.

Bottom and horizontal safe-area insets are kept (for home indicator + landscape notch). The topbar now sits tight to Obsidian's chrome.
releases.name: 0.14.3 — tighten mobile top spacing

releases.author: wesswart77
releases.description:
## 📱 Final mobile polish

Wraps up the mobile rollout that started in v0.14.0 with the bits that make Cadence feel native on iPhone and iPad rather than just "works on a phone".

### What's new
- **Safe-area aware** — the top bar respects the iPhone notch and Dynamic Island, the nav drawer respects the home indicator. No more controls hiding behind hardware.
- **Compact Top of the Day** — on phones, the briefing card shows the 3 most urgent items (overdue → time-sensitive → opportunities) with a "+N more" hint pointing to the cards below. On desktop / tablet in landscape, the full briefing is unchanged.
- **Touch-only button visibility** — across every surface (kanban, milestones, inbox, project tasks), the action buttons that used to appear only on hover are now persistently visible on touch devices. Applies to iPad in landscape too.

### Same behaviour on desktop
Nothing about the desktop experience changes. Wider screens get the full layout, full briefing, hover-revealed controls — all as it was.

That completes the mobile rollout. Cadence on iPhone now feels like an app, not a desktop tool squeezed onto a phone.
releases.name: 0.14.2 — mobile polish

releases.author: wesswart77
releases.description:
## 📱 Mobile touch polish

Follow-up to v0.14.0's mobile layout work — now the kanban actually works on touch devices.

### What changed
- **Kanban on iPhone/iPad** — drag-to-move is disabled on touch devices (HTML5 drag-drop never reliably fires from a touch screen). Instead, tap a card to open its detail and change the stage via the dropdown there. The detail view auto-saves and the kanban updates.
- **Desktop kanban is untouched** — drag still works as it always has.
- **Subtler touch cues** — kanban cards on mobile show a pointer cursor instead of grab, no hover lift (which doesn't translate to touch).

### Why
v0.14.0 made the layout responsive but kanban drag-drop was broken on iOS Safari. This closes that gap.
releases.name: 0.14.1 — mobile touch fixes

releases.author: wesswart77
releases.description:
## 📱 Cadence on iPhone and iPad

Cadence is now properly responsive — open it on your phone or tablet and the whole app reshapes itself.

### What's new on mobile
- **Hamburger nav drawer** — left rail slides in/out, taps backdrop to dismiss
- **Single-column layouts everywhere** — Home, Project Detail, Dashboard, Reports
- **Finger-friendly buttons** — 36px min height, generous padding
- **Action buttons stay visible on touch** — delete ✕, project-link 📁, reminder bell 🔔 no longer hide behind hover
- **Modals go full-width** — New Deal, Edit Reminder, CSV Import all become near-full-screen on phones
- **Page headers stack** — title on top, action buttons wrap below
- **Tighter spacing** throughout to make the most of small screens

### Same data, different shape
Nothing about your vault changes. Open Cadence on desktop — same as ever. Open it on iPhone — feels like a phone app.

### Tested on
- iPhone (Obsidian Mobile)
- Obsidian desktop on narrow window widths (the same media queries kick in below 760px wide)

Rolling out via the community plugin portal once it picks up this release.
releases.name: 0.14.0 — Cadence on mobile

releases.author: wesswart77
releases.description:
## 🔒 Behind the scenes

Two improvements driven by the Obsidian community plugin portal's review:

### Scoped file reads
Cadence previously called `vault.getMarkdownFiles()` to find entities, which technically gives the plugin a view of every file path in your vault. Now it walks only the specific entity folders it owns (`Cadence/Contacts/`, `Cadence/Projects/`, etc.). Faster on large vaults, less invasive.

The one exception is the **CSV import file picker** — that intentionally lists `.csv` files anywhere so you can import from wherever you've put them. Clearly commented as such in the source.

### Verified release provenance
Every release from this version onwards is **cryptographically attested via GitHub** — anyone can verify that `main.js`, `styles.css`, and `manifest.json` you download from a release were built from this exact source repo at this exact commit. The community plugin portal surfaces this as a verified-provenance signal.

No behaviour changes. Existing data, settings, and integrations all keep working.
releases.name: 0.13.12 — privacy + provenance

releases.author: wesswart77
releases.description:
## ✨ What's new

### Top of the Day — assistant-style daily briefing
The Home view now opens with a smart briefing card that surfaces what actually needs your attention today — pulled from data Cadence already tracks. Each row is clickable and jumps you to the relevant surface.

It looks at:
- 🎯 Open tasks on today's note
- ⚠ Overdue reminders (with a preview of the first one)
- ⏰ Reminders due later today
- 💼 Deals closing this week — count + total value
- 👤 Stale contacts on open deals (no contact in 30+ days)
- 📅 Project milestones due within 14 days
- 🎉 Deals you won this week — small celebration moment

The headline adapts to context ("A couple of things need attention this morning" if anything's overdue, otherwise "Here's what's on your radar"). Empty state is friendly too — "Inbox zero. Clear runway."

### Why
Opening Cadence should feel valuable in the first two seconds. The briefing turns Home from a passive landing page into a personal assistant that points at what's slipping, closing, or worth a small win.

### Notes
- All computed locally from your vault data — no AI, no network calls
- Color-coded left borders (rose / emerald / sky / mint / warn) signal urgency
- Works in both light and dark Cadence modes
releases.name: 0.13.11 — Top of the Day

releases.author: wesswart77
releases.description: Adds `fundingUrl` to manifest pointing at https://www.buymeacoffee.com/wesswart77 — Obsidian's community plugin browser will now show a heart icon next to Cadence that opens the support page.
releases.name: 0.13.10 — Buy Me a Coffee link

releases.author: wesswart77
releases.description:
Addresses CSS lint warnings flagged by the Obsidian community directory automated review:

- Removed all `!important` declarations. Selector specificity is now used to handle override cases:
  - Dark-mode milestone/task input rules (3-class selector already outranks base 1-class rules)
  - `.cad-create-input.cad-import-select` chained-class selector
  - `.cad-dash-card-body.cad-mini-stat-row` chained-class selector
- Merged duplicate `.cad-kanban-col` and `.cad-kanban-col-list` selectors into single declarations
- No behavioural change — purely a CSS health fix
releases.name: 0.13.9 — CSS lint fixes

releases.author: wesswart77
releases.description:
**Task ↔ project linking**
- Every task in Today and Home Today tile now has a 📁 link button — pick a project, get a chip you can click to jump straight to that project's detail
- Reminder edit modal has a new PROJECT field — link/change/remove a project from any reminder via Obsidian's suggester
- Linked tasks show an emerald 'Project Name' chip inline

**Dark mode visibility fix**
- Milestone titles, dates, and task titles were going white-on-white in dark mode on hover/focus. Now stay dark with light text at all states.
- Cadence-app dark mode now uses Obsidian's neutral dark palette directly and inherits live surface variables when Obsidian is also in dark mode, so the pane blends seamlessly with whichever Obsidian dark theme you have on.

**Typography**
- Switched from Geist to Manrope — softer, premium SaaS feel. Inter stays as the fallback if Google Fonts isn't reachable. JetBrains Mono still owns numerics.
releases.name: 0.13.8 — task↔project links, dark mode visibility, Manrope

releases.author: wesswart77
releases.description:
Cadence-app dark mode now uses Obsidian's neutral dark palette (matches default Obsidian dark hex-for-hex) instead of warm Cadence grays.

When Obsidian is *also* in dark mode, the Cadence app inherits Obsidian's live surface variables — so the Cadence pane blends seamlessly with whatever Obsidian dark theme you have active (default, Minimal, Things, etc.). When Obsidian is in light mode and you flip Cadence to dark, the explicit Obsidian-default-dark colors are used.
releases.name: 0.13.7 — dark mode tuned to Obsidian

releases.author: wesswart77
releases.description: Adds an in-app dark mode for the Cadence workspace, scoped to `.cadence-app` only — your Obsidian frame, sidebar, and other plugins stay on whatever theme/mode you have set globally. Sun/moon icon in the top bar toggles. Persisted per vault.
releases.name: 0.13.6 — Cadence-app dark mode

releases.author: wesswart77
releases.description: Adds a sun/moon icon in the top bar to toggle Obsidian's light/dark mode without leaving the Cadence app.
releases.name: 0.13.5 — dark mode toggle

releases.author: wesswart77
releases.description:
First public release of Cadence.

A workspace for working life in Obsidian:
- Home command centre
- CRM: Dashboard, Pipeline (drag-to-stage kanban), Contacts, Companies, Activities
- PRM: Partners, Registrations, Commissions, Leads, Certifications, Analytics
- Planner: Today, Calendar (week), Projects (rich PM view with milestones + notes), Inbox with reminders that fire
- Reports: Pipeline, Sales, Partners, Activity, Productivity
- CSV import for any entity type
- Module toggles (CRM / PRM / Planner)
- Markdown source-of-truth, no server, no sync service
releases.name: 0.13.4 — initial submission
